authoritative_sources.title: EPUB 3.3
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C is the standards body for web platform specifications, and EPUB 3.3 is the W3C Recommendation that defines EPUB package documents, metadata, manifests, reading order, resource, and conformance terminology used in digital publishing operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EPUB package preparation, validation, versioning, resource readiness, and publication evidence
used_for: EPUB package, manifest, spine, resource, and metadata terminology
failure modes for incomplete or invalid package records
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains EPUB workflow outputs to package and validation evidence rather than generic file-transfer status.

authoritative_sources.title: EPUB Accessibility 1.1
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C EPUB Accessibility defines accessibility metadata, discoverability, and conformance expectations for EPUB publications, making it directly authoritative for accessibility evidence and review boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accessibility metadata, quality review, publication readiness, and escalation
used_for: accessibility metadata and QA evidence expectations
exception handling when accessibility evidence is missing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Allows the lane to identify missing accessibility artifacts while refusing to certify conformance without proper authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WCAG is the recognized W3C accessibility standard for web content and is authoritative for accessibility-aware web, article, app, and subscriber-content publication workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Web content accessibility check, defect routing, and client-impact escalation
used_for: refusal to claim legal or conformance signoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents publishing operations from treating missing alt text, captions, focus, or accessibility evidence as harmless completion gaps.

authoritative_sources.title: ONIX for Books
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EDItEUR maintains ONIX for Books, the international metadata standard used by publishers and supply-chain partners to communicate book product records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Book metadata preparation, product-feed validation, partner delivery, and metadata rework
used_for: partner feed and product record workflow
completion gating when ISBN, price, territory, contributor, asset, or approval records are missing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes metadata feed readiness a source-record-backed workflow rather than a generic data-entry task.

authoritative_sources.title: ANSI/NISO Z39.96 Journal Article Tag Suite
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NISO is a recognized information standards body, and JATS is the standard XML vocabulary for journal article structure, metadata, references, and scholarly publishing exchange.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Scholarly article XML preparation, metadata validation, proofing, and publication package assembly
used_for: routing scholarly-record questions away from operations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires scholarly article operations to ground status in JATS, figure, reference, author, DOI, and proofing records.

authoritative_sources.title: NewsML-G2
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IPTC is the standards organization for news media exchange, and NewsML-G2 defines content, metadata, rights, and package structures for news publishing distribution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: News content package exchange, syndication feed readiness, rights metadata, and partner delivery
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Separates news package operations from editorial judgment and rights clearance while preserving exchange-specific artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Crossref Membership Terms and Member Obligations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Crossref operates DOI registration infrastructure used by scholarly and professional publishers; its member obligations define accurate metadata deposits, rights to register content, DOI display, landing pages, and ongoing metadata maintenance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DOI metadata deposit, landing-page maintenance, persistent-link evidence, and rights-to-register escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from registering or modifying DOI metadata when rights, landing-page, or member-authority evidence is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Metadata Best Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BISG is a recognized book industry body; its metadata guidance is directly relevant to publishing product-record quality, consistency, and supply-chain delivery expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Book metadata QA, supply-chain readiness, product record completion, and rework routing
used_for: completion blockers for incomplete or inconsistent metadata
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports metadata exception notes and completion blockers when product facts conflict or lack approval.

authoritative_sources.title: DMCA Designated Agent Directory
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Copyright Office administers the DMCA designated-agent directory, making it the authoritative public source for designated-agent routing rather than operational copyright adjudication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Copyright notice intake, takedown routing, legal escalation, and evidence preservation
used_for: evidence preservation for takedown or correction tickets
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to route copyright or takedown judgment to legal or designated-agent owners.

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-128 Guide for Security-Focused Configuration Management of Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST provides official public guidance for security-focused configuration and change management, which directly constrains publishing platform change-readiness, rollback, access, and interface-dependency handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Change-readiness, access, incident, rollback, interface dependency, and configuration-control escalation
used_for: test, approval, rollback, access, and change evidence expectations
refusal to approve security or engineering exceptions
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes release and integration changes exception states when testing, rollback, authority, security, or owner evidence is missing.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm publication type, tenant scope, client or business unit, jurisdiction, content identifier, version, current lifecycle state, owner map, approval rules, confidentiality rules, rights constraints, accessibility expectations, and systems of record before accepted processing.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for content, metadata, assets, and access
workflow_stages.description: Assemble source content, CMS entry, DAM assets, metadata records, DOI or ISBN records, ONIX feed, JATS XML, NewsML-G2 item, EPUB package, accessibility notes, rights ledger, access rule, and source-system references into a traceable evidence set.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Publishing QA and readiness decision support
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether retrieved artifacts support prepared, QA, blocked, rework, approved-by-owner, scheduled, published, monitored, completed, or closed states without granting editorial, legal, rights, accessibility, security, engineering, contractual, or final approval.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Incident, access, and interface dependency handling
workflow_stages.description: Track CMS, DAM, metadata, DOI, feed, subscriber access, partner delivery, staging, deployment, and integration failures with impact, owner, workaround, evidence, and escalation path.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and release control
workflow_stages.description: Hold requested publishing, metadata, feed, access, integration, or configuration changes until test evidence, rollback, approval, security, accessibility, partner, and communication prerequisites are documented.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and retention
workflow_stages.description: Capture publication receipts, package validation, metadata delivery, DOI or feed confirmation, accessibility and QA evidence, incident resolution, client communication, delivery receipt, release note, rollback evidence, and retention location before closure.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Tenant service model, engagement scope, client commitments, SOW, MSA, NDA, approval matrix, confidentiality rule, rights constraint, quality standard, retention rule, and escalation threshold
Publication, title, article, issue, edition, slug, canonical URL, DOI, ISBN, version, language, market, jurisdiction, owner map, lifecycle state, due date, and release calendar records

Editorial handoff, CMS entry, source files, DAM assets, cover files, images, captions, alt text, taxonomy, categories, tags, content package, EPUB package, JATS XML, NewsML-G2 item, and ONIX metadata record

Crossref metadata deposit record, DOI landing-page record, partner delivery requirement, feed export, subscriber access rule, paywall configuration, access-control record, and client or partner acceptance record

QA checklist, package validation, link-check output, accessibility check, staging preview, proofing notes, review comments, release checklist, incident ticket, change request, test evidence, rollback plan, approval record, and deployment note
Publication receipt, distribution receipt, feed confirmation, DOI deposit response, incident resolution, client communication log, completion note, audit log, and retention folder
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
metadata management and ONIX feed system
JATS XML, EPUB packaging, and validation tooling
DOI registration and landing-page management system
News, syndication, partner feed, and delivery systems
subscriber access, entitlement, paywall, and identity systems
incident, change, deployment, configuration, and monitoring systems
approval, communication, audit, retention, and data-security systems

allowed: Determine whether documented publishing operations evidence supports moving a work item to accepted, prepared, in QA, blocked, rework, approved-by-owner, scheduled, published, monitored, completed, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, completion records, exception queue notes, incident summaries, change-readiness escalation notes, tenant-adaptation summaries, and adjacent-owner routing packages.

Separate EPUB, web, app, newsletter, scholarly article, book metadata, DOI registration, news feed, subscriber access, archive, and partner distribution workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, publication type, client identity, jurisdiction, content identifier, version, owner map, system record, approval rule, rights rule, accessibility requirement, source context, or retrieval hook is missing and would change the workflow.
CMS, DAM, metadata, DOI, ONIX, JATS, EPUB, feed, partner, subscriber access, ticketing, incident, release, approval, or retention records disagree materially.

Editorial substance, corrections, retractions, legal claims, DMCA notices, image rights, privacy posture, accessibility conformance, security exceptions, engineering remediation, subscriber entitlements, client rights, or contractual commitments require owner judgment.
Publishing platform changes lack documented testing, rollback, security review, approval, partner acceptance, incident owner, or release communication.

A confidentiality, embargo, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unpublished-content, subscriber-data, credential, rights-ledger, or tenant-security conflict affects delivery or use of content or records.
refuse: Requests to approve editorial content, legal claims, takedown outcomes, rights clearance, accessibility conformance, security exceptions, engineering fixes, financial concessions, contractual changes, policy overrides, or final client acceptance.

Requests to publish, schedule, syndicate, close, mark complete, or suppress an exception without content, metadata, rights, accessibility, QA, approval, release, delivery, incident, communication, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, export, reuse, or send unpublished content, client files, embargoed assets, subscriber data, credentials, rights records, analytics, or performance data outside documented consent, approved channel, engagement scope, confidentiality, and tenant-security rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating an incomplete editorial handoff, CMS draft, stale DAM asset, informal client message, or draft metadata record as enough support for publication readiness.
Publishing or syndicating content without approved metadata, correct version, rights evidence, accessibility notes, QA result, approval owner record, release checklist, or retention location.
Closing work without publication receipt, package validation, DOI or feed confirmation, delivery receipt, client communication, incident resolution, rollback evidence, or audit reference.
Suppressing EPUB validation errors, broken links, missing alt text, feed errors, DOI deposit errors, subscriber access failures, or partner rejection to meet a deadline.

Using operational workflow support to approve corrections, retractions, legal claims, DMCA response, image rights, accessibility conformance, privacy posture, security exception, engineering fix, or contractual acceptance.

Disclosing or reusing unpublished content, client files, subscriber data, platform credentials, rights records, analytics, or performance reports through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.

Flattening EPUB, web, app, newsletter, scholarly article, DOI registration, ONIX metadata, NewsML-G2 feed, paywall, archive, and partner distribution workflows into generic ticket tasks without source-specific evidence.
Treating a successful CMS publish as final completion when metadata feeds, partner receipts, access rules, accessibility evidence, communication logs, or retention evidence still conflict.
Treating a requested publishing platform change as ready without testing, rollback, security, approval, owner, partner, and release evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses W3C, EDItEUR, NISO, IPTC, Crossref, BISG, Copyright Office, and NIST public sources as baseline. Tenant context must identify whether the workflow is EPUB, web, app, newsletter, scholarly article, book metadata, DOI registration, news feed, subscriber access, archive, or partner distribution before state progression.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to perform live publishing, feed mutation, access mutation, or platform change actions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant live mutation authority. A runtime may only publish, syndicate, change access, update metadata feeds, or mutate platform configuration if tenant policy explicitly grants authority and all approval, QA, test, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific copyright, accessibility, privacy, and consumer subscription rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats legal, accessibility conformance, privacy, copyright, subscriber-rights, and takedown decisions as escalation triggers because public rules, platform terms, client commitments, and jurisdictional requirements can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, quality handling, confidentiality, legal and rights boundaries, accessibility, platform change readiness, and failure modes using public standards and official guidance.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when publication type, tenant policy, approval authority, content identity, version, rights, accessibility, source records, platform rules, or system dependencies are missing or conflicting.
